title: Object 8
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Object 8: "Magic Door Knobs"}} == Description ==
[[File:SIZED+793+Antique+Brass+Handle.JPG|300px]]
A pair of unused Magic Door Knobs in the custody of the M.E.G.
[[File:SIZED+793+Antique+Brass+Handle.JPG|600px]]
{{CSS|content= }} Object 8, also known as '''Magic Door Knobs,''' are very rare objects found in all levels of the Backrooms, regardless of their nature. They resemble standard brass passage door knobs but lack the parts necessary to mechanically function as one (such as the chassis or spindle), consisting only the knob and backplate. A thin groove secures the backplate to the knob's end, allowing the knob to spin freely within the backplate. The knobs are spherical in shape and are smooth by the touch, devoid of any engraved design on their surfaces. When a '''Magic Door Knob''' is placed on a wall that is at least 36 inches by 80 inches large with adequate flatness, it will adhere to the wall immediately and be secured in place. Once fixed to a wall, they cannot be physically removed without inflicting enough damage to render them unusable. When the installed '''Magic Door Knob '''is turned 90 degrees, regardless of direction, it will lock in place and cannot be rotated. Once pulled, it instantly transforms a 36"x80" portion of the wall into a functional door. The “door” created from '''Magic Door Knobs''' will feature a door frame that matches the material and three brass hinges on one side. Which side the hinges are located is dependent on the '''Magic Door Knob’s''' location on the entire wall (See Testing/Additional Information). Both the frame and the hinges are impossible to be removed or destroyed. What lies behind doors created by '''Magic Door Knobs''' is determined by their location and will lead to any level in the Backrooms containing a suitable wall. What levels link to these doors does not appear to follow a pattern, but it will be the same for all doors within a specific radius. Once a living entity has fully entered the door and is no longer being observed, the door will automatically close shut and vanish along with the '''Magic Door Knob''', returning the portion of the wall to its original state. Despite their seeming commonality, '''Magic Door Knobs''' are never found on doors, although they can be found in a variety of other places; some have even been spotted lying in the center of empty, open areas. There is insufficient data to indicate that they occur in greater abundance in specific levels. The prevalent belief that there are more '''Magic Door Knobs''' on levels containing doors is somewhat illogical, as they cannot be found on doors. It is unknown whether they manifest similarly to [[Object 1|Almond Water]], or there is a finite amount of them within the backrooms. == Usage == Due to their single-time use and rarity, '''Magic Door Knobs '''are extremely valuable to all wanderers of the Backrooms. They are typically utilized in any extreme emergencies when an escape route is required and absolutely no other options are currently available; '''Magic Door Knobs''' are never used frivolously because collecting more of them is incredibly time-consuming. There is, however, the possibility of entering a level that is even more dangerous than the current one. Because destinations are impossible to determine even with doors created in the same levels, this further encourages the use of '''Magic Door Knobs''' only when absolutely necessary. '''Magic Door Knobs''' are notoriously counterfeited due to the presence of similar, traditional door knobs in the Backrooms. As there is no obvious difference between them and other door knobs, traditional door knobs can easily be misidentified as actual '''Magic Door Knobs'''. There have been numerous reports of wanderers removing ordinary door knobs off doors on levels with doors, such as Levels [[Level 4|4]] and [[Level 5|5]], and selling them on colony marketplaces for a price comparable to the market value of genuine '''Magic Door Knobs'''. This can be difficult to detect as a standard consumer because testing must be avoided to prevent wasting potentially real Magic Door Knobs by placing them on walls.
[[File:DoorKnobs_Educational_Poster.png|300px]]
An Educational Poster on identifying real and counterfeit Magic Door Knobs.
[[File:DoorKnobs_Educational_Poster.png|600px]]
The only way to recognize counterfeit door knobs is to learn the exact appearance of genuine '''Magic Door Knobs'''; fortunately, this is simple because all '''Magic Door Knobs''' look exactly the same: * Both the knob and the backplate are made entirely of brass. * The knob is spherical, shiny, and smooth. * The backplate has three evenly distant screw holes forming a triangle. Each hole is 6mm in diameter. * The backplate is thin and circular in shape. * The hole in the center of the backplate (where the spindle would fit) is square-shaped.
Notice If you or someone you know has encountered the sale, salvaging, or trading of counterfeit '''Magic Door Knobs''', please notify the nearest M.E.G outpost.
During their discovery, '''Magic Door Knobs''' were simply known as "Door Knobs" until wanderers began counterfeiting them in colony marketplaces. The name soon had to be changed to verbally distinguish them from traditional door knobs. Colonies soon had to crack down on any operations involving the fraud of fake '''Magic Door Knobs'''. Counterfeiting has decreased significantly from its peak in 2005, to the point where purchasing '''Magic Door Knobs''' on the market is no longer an unacceptable risk. Many travelers have learned to tell apart '''Magic Door Knobs''' thanks to informative posters, and other miscellaneous sources.

“'''Magic Door Knob''' counterfeiting was so common that the concept of '''Magic Door Knobs ''' became more about the fraud than what the things could actually do. People who never even seen or used a real '''Magic Door Knob''' began to believe that it was all a giant scam developed by fraudulent traders to easily win a large trade off. It was sad that such a useful object was turned into a symbol of dishonesty in the Backrooms. Authority must be used to potentially restore faith in the procuring of '''Magic Door Knobs.'''”

— ''Dan Calloway, M.E.G Officer''

==Testing / Additional Information == ''The following information is based on limited testing conducted by M.E.G. researchers. Tests were carried out non-meticulously since a sufficient supply of '''Magic Door Knobs''' were still needed for emergencies. Because of the limited supply of the objects, each experiment required a precise procedure to get as much value from each '''Magic Door Knob''' as feasible.'' The minimum appropriate surface area required for '''Magic Door Knobs''' to successfully adhere to the wall is only equal to the dimensions of the backplate. Such surfaces require roughly at most 4mm of flatness deviation. While flatness is only necessary for the region where the '''Magic Door Knob''' will be attached, the 36"x80" section of wall must still have similar geometric properties of a flat wall in order to split from the rest of the wall and become a door; sections of wall that have large bends or corners will not work with '''Magic Door Knobs'''. So far, testing has not been done on walls with holes, although it is expected that they will not work if the size of the hole is significant enough. {| class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed fandom-table" style="float: right" |+ Window View Outcomes !% of all or # of experiments ! Outcome |- | 50% || Pure black. |- |20% |A wall identical to the one used in the experiments. |- |10% |Pure white. |- |2 Experiments |the level on the other side of the door could be seen. |- |1 Experiment |Pure Red. |- |1 Experiment |A brick wall. Unrelated to both the level experimented in and the level where the door lead to. |- |1 Experiment |[REDACTED], door lead to Level ▋▋▋. |} Walls that include windows will work with '''Magic Door Knobs''' most of the time60% success rate. Research into what and why certain windows work in experiments is in consideration., but only if the window in its entirety fits within the 36"x80" section. Windows affected by '''Magic Door Knobs''' have shown a variety of observable results, which are closely related to what is seen through the window. Other things implanted in walls, such as outlets, switches, vents, and so on, will not interfere with the creation of doors from '''Magic Door Knobs'''. Such objects can be divided if a part of them is outside of the section selected to convert into a door. '''Magic Door Knobs''' are capable of transforming practically any wall, regardless of material, into a functioning door. How exactly it cuts out sections of wall to achieve this is unknown. The cuts made to create the 36"x80" section of wall are perfectly straight and almost seamless, sometimes unnoticeable until the door is opened. The weight of the doors created from '''Magic Door Knobs''' matches that of the 36"x80" piece of wall used. This may make heavier walls more difficult to move; however, the door hinges appear to compensate by being almost frictionless~0.03-0.07\mu_s / ~0.02-0.05\mu_k. The arrangement of the 36"x80" door is determined by both the floor of the room, and the horizontal placement of the '''Magic Door Knob'''. '''Magic Door Knobs''' may be placed at any height, as long as it does not exceed ~78~2 inches to compensate for the size of the Magic Door Knob's backplate inches from the floor. If the '''Magic Door Knob''' is positioned closer to one end of the wall where it was placed, the generated 36"x80" door will pivot on the opposite side, with the '''Magic Door Knob''' located near the side of the door closest to the end of the wall. The neighboring side will be precisely 2-3/4 inches from the center of the '''Magic Door Knob'''.Common backset measurement (Distance between the edge of the door and the center of the door knob hole) Attempts to position '''Magic Door Knobs''' absolutely center on the wall have produced unsatisfying results, since it appears to judge closeness down to incredibly small levels. Using '''Magic Door Knobs''' on walls experiencing any form of velocity will start to demonstrate instability. This becomes more noticeable when the wall's velocity is varied by acceleration. Doors created on such walls may still be entered, but it is highly dangerous and the outcomes are unpredictable. In most cases, there is still a link to a different level, but the door on the other side presents a variety of reactions: * The door may “move” on the wall, progressively changing its location without damaging any of the wall. * The door may disappear and reappear at irregular intervals. * Shadows and colors may appear distorted. * A person could fully enter through the door, but will ultimately vanish. It’s unknown where they end up, but it's presumably [[The Void]] as they've never been located. * Behind the door may show complete darkness. * Only parts of the door will appear. * [DATA EXPUNGED] 100% casualty rate. * The door will appear “glitchy”, with sudden movements and changes in opaqueness. * The door will appear clipped through the ceiling or floor. * Behind the door shows the original wall. This may be the same for the side of the door where the Magic Door Knob was used. {{Collapse|initial='''> ''''''Open Author Information'''|final='''> ''''''Close Author Information'''|body= '''Author''': [[User:lux n|Lux3n]] |num=9}}
